title: Fluid Dynamics & Turbulence: Kolmogorov Cascade & NS Regularity

k41.why_eml2: Power law = exp(α·log k) = paired exp+log = EML-2 by definition
k41.type_of_depth_change: TYPE 2: Horizon shadow (coarse-graining reveals EML-2 structure)

kolmogorov_microscale.why: Ratio of viscous to inertial scales = log combination of physical parameters

structure_function.why: Power law in r = exp((p/3)log r) = EML-2

multifractal.singularity_spectrum: D(h): Hausdorff dimension of set with Hölder exponent h

intermittency.key_insight: Intermittency = EML-2 average + EML-3 fluctuations. The smooth K41 cascade is EML-2; the singular vortex filaments push toward EML-3. Full intermittent turbulence = EML-3 (oscillatory multifractal corrections).

regularity.why_eml_inf: Full NS: nonlinear self-interaction + pressure nonlocality = EML-∞

energy_inequality.why: L² energy = integral of squared norms = EML-2 (measurement shadow)

shadow_structure.secondary_shadow: EML-3 (explicit Fourier modes of vortex stretching)
shadow_structure.millennium_status: EML-∞ whether or not blow-up exists; both outcomes = EML-∞ statement

three_types_analysis.type_1_finite: Energy cascade K41: Δd=−∞ shadow at EML-2
three_types_analysis.type_2_horizon: Potential blow-up: EML-3→EML-∞ via vortex stretching runaway
three_types_analysis.type_3_categorification: NOT applicable to NS directly (no enrichment step)

2d_case.global_regularity: Proved: 2D NS is globally regular, EML-2 forever

3d_case.transition_type: TYPE 2 Horizon: smooth EML-3 solution → EML-∞ singular

enstrophy.blow_up: Ω → ∞ in finite time = Horizon crossing (EML-2 quantity becomes EML-∞)

ns_regularity.key_insight: NS Millennium Problem through Three Types: TYPE 1 (finite Δd): K41 cascade = Δd=-∞ shadow at EML-2. TYPE 2 (Horizon): potential blow-up = EML-3 → EML-∞ via vortex stretching runaway. TYPE 3 (Categorification): not directly applicable. The EML-2 shadow (energy inequality) is what numerical methods compute. The EML-∞ question (blow-up) is the Millennium Prize.

key_theorem: The Three Depth-Change Types Applied to Turbulence (S238): The Kolmogorov -5/3 law is EML-2: E(k) ~ k^{-5/3} is a power law = exp((-5/3)log k), the canonical log-log scaling = EML-2 by the log-integral equivalence (Direction B). The jump NS(EML-∞) → K41(EML-2) is a TYPE 2 Horizon shadow (Δd=-∞, coarse-graining). Intermittency corrections elevate K41 from EML-2 to EML-3 (multifractal oscillations). NS blow-up, if it occurs, is a TYPE 2 Horizon crossing: smooth EML-3 → singular EML-∞. The 2D NS global regularity theorem is the statement that without vortex stretching, the system stays at EML-2 forever — confirming EML-3 closure requires the 3D stretching term.
rabbit_hole_log: -5/3 law: E(k)~k^{-5/3} = exp((-5/3)log k) = EML-2 by log-integral equivalence
NS blow-up = TYPE 2 Horizon crossing: vortex stretching drives EML-3 → EML-∞
2D regularity: no stretching → stays EML-2 forever; 3D = open Horizon question
